title: Campium gaboonense
intro: Campium gaboonense, also known as the Gaboon fern or Campium fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical forests of Central and West Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Campium gaboonense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, or dried and made into capsules or tablets, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections, diarrhea, and skin conditions.
